reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 2 (Trust Boundary) triggered. The finding correctly identifies that line 148 of compute_fork_digest_post_fulu uses blob_entry.epoch (the schedule entry's activation epoch) instead of the current epoch parameter, which deviates from the spec requirement in outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W0B1_1771748647.json line 82 ('fork_digest(e) == trunc4(fork_data_root(e) XOR hash(blob_params(e)))'). However, the attack scenario requires nodes to be deployed with different blob_schedule configurations. The blob_schedule is part of the hardcoded network consensus configuration (see types/src/config.rs:512-517 for mainnet config). All honest nodes on the same network must use identical blob_schedule values to achieve consensus. An attacker cannot cause nodes to use different blob_schedule values; this would require the node operator to use incorrect/mismatched configuration files. According to BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json trust_assumptions.local_validator: 'Local validator configuration is under operator control' with 'in_scope_for_bug_bounty: false' and note 'Configuration issues requiring operator error are typically out-of-scope'. While this is a spec compliance deviation, it is not exploitable via attacker-controlled input and requires operator misconfiguration.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Fork digest must incorporate blob parameters; mismatched digests cause network partition. Assertion: forall epoch e: fork_digest(e) == trunc4(fork_data_root(e) XOR hash(blob_params(e)))
